query: France Ebola case 2026: current status of the doctor who tested positive after aid mission, any secondary cases, ECDC reporting. How many confirmed Ebola cases in France as of July 2026?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the key answer to your question:

**As of July 2026, there is exactly 1 confirmed Ebola case in France.**

- **The patient**: A humanitarian doctor with the NGO ALIMA who tested positive on June 24, 2026, after returning (via commercial Air France flight, arriving June 23) from an aid mission in the DRC. This was the first Ebola case ever confirmed on French/European soil and the first to test positive outside Africa during the 2026 Bundibugyo outbreak.
- **Current status**: **Recovered and discharged from hospital on July 4, 2026**, after two negative PCR tests (announced by Health Minister Stéphanie Rist). The doctor was nearly asymptomatic (only headaches).
- **Secondary cases**: **None.** Five fellow flight passengers were identified as contacts and placed in precautionary isolation, but no onward transmission occurred. The case was described as "successfully contained."
- **ECDC reporting**: Active monitoring with multiple publications (importation-risk estimates, contact-tracing guidance, threat assessment brief, imported-case preparedness updated July 10, 2026). ECDC assesses the overall EU/EEA population risk as **"very low"** (~1 imported case per 24,000 travelers from the outbreak region). WHO declared the outbreak a PHEIC on May 17, 2026.

The full synthesis with all sources and data points has been recorded in the structured output.

sources.title: France: first Ebola case 'recovered' and 'discharged from hospital'
sources.snippet: Health Minister Stéphanie Rist announced France's first diagnosed Ebola case has recovered after two negative PCR tests and was discharged from hospital; five flight contacts isolated as precaution.

sources.snippet: France confirmed its first Ebola case — a doctor returning from a humanitarian mission in the DRC tested positive; first Ebola case confirmed in Europe.

sources.title: Ebola response worker who traveled from DRC to France is first in outbreak to test positive outside of Africa
sources.snippet: First reported case in France and first to test positive outside Africa; outbreak driven by rare Bundibugyo strain.

sources.title: France confirms first Ebola case in doctor returning from DR Congo
sources.snippet: Patient in isolation following strict biosafety protocols to prevent spread in France.

sources.title: Ebola Arrives in France: Infected Aid Doctor Flew Commercial as 1,118 DRC Cases Mount
sources.snippet: First-ever Ebola case on French territory June 24, 2026; ALIMA physician who flew commercial; DRC cases mounting.

sources.title: The First European Ebola Case in Years Was Successfully Contained in France
sources.snippet: French ALIMA doctor stepped off flight at Charles de Gaulle June 23; case successfully contained.

sources.title: Estimation of the importation risk of Bundibugyo virus into the EU/EEA in June 2026
sources.snippet: ECDC estimates probability of importation of Bundibugyo virus into EU/EEA countries for 11-25 June 2026.

sources.title: Preparedness and response for imported cases of Ebola disease into an EU/EEA country
sources.snippet: Updated 10 July 2026; overall risk of BDBV Ebola for general EU/EEA population assessed as very low; ~1 imported case per 24,000 travelers.

sources.title: Ebola disease outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda
sources.snippet: As of 10 July 2026, DRC reported 1,792 confirmed cases in the Bundibugyo virus outbreak affecting DRC and Uganda.

sources.title: 2026 Ebola epidemic
sources.snippet: 17th DRC Ebola outbreak, Bundibugyo strain; 702 confirmed deaths in DRC and two in Uganda as of second week of July; imported case reported in France.
